sanskrit:
तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः।
विस्मयो मे महान् राजन् हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः॥
transliteration:
tac ca saṃsmṛtya saṃsmṛtya rūpam aty-adbhutaṃ hareḥ |
vismayo me mahān rājan hṛṣyāmi ca punaḥ punaḥ ||
literal_meaning: That—again and again remembering—the exceedingly wonderful form of Hari—great wonder (is) mine, O King; I thrill again and again.
contextual_meaning: Sañjaya, recounting the vision to Dhṛtarāṣṭra, describes how the mind, once exposed to a transpersonal archetype of wholeness (Kṛṣṇa’s viśvarūpa), keeps returning to it, producing awe and repeated joy. The verse models post-peak-experience integration: the psyche loops the memory, each replay rekindling affect, suggesting that numinous encounters become self-reinforcing inner resources.
psychological_state: Post-peak integration state: intrusive re-living of a transpersonal vision that simultaneously delights and destabilizes ordinary self-structure.
trigger_scenario: After a life-changing psychedelic therapy session or a near-death experience, the client cannot stop mentally replaying the light-filled imagery, feeling both ecstatic and slightly unmoored.
anchor_text: Let the memory of awe rekindle your joy whenever the mind returns to it.
